what: SuperX (X/Twitter growth tool for SaaS/dev agencies): Hit $23k MRR in 6 months post-launch (from $1k day 1), ~650 customers at $39/mo, 20-25% MoM growth. Founder reversed viral X posts into product after agency pivot.
why_us: Proves demand for Twitter-centric growth tools in indie/SaaS space; shows fast path to meaningful revenue for X-leveraged products. Opportunity to build complementary automation or AI features.

what: AI orchestration platform (Meerkats.ai) reached $3k+ MRR in 4 weeks in crowded market. AI design tool hit $10k MRR in 6 weeks with no marketing spend.
why_us: Validates quick traction for focused AI wrappers/orchestrators. Signals opportunity in niche AI automation for specific workflows (e.g., design, ops) where context/integration is key pain.

what: Frustrations with SaaS alternatives, pricing, and missing features (e.g., Trustpilot alts, resume/CV tools vs ChatGPT, booking/niche tools). Common wishes for cheaper, simpler, or more integrated options. X posts on 'I wish there was' and building for specific niches (salons/gyms).
why_us: Recurring pain in reviews/forums around cost, bloat, and integration. High potential for micro-SaaS filling gaps (e.g., niche booking, review collection, context-heavy AI).

what: Indie builders sharing quick wins: Booking demo for local businesses (salons etc.) → $300-$2.5k one-time + $100-500/mo retainers. AI wrappers and outreach automation pains (stitching tools). Calls for reliable single-prompt workflows.
why_us: Concrete monetization path for automation bots/tools targeting SMBs. Low competition in hyper-niche verticals; repeatable playbook.

what: Habit tracking app (consumer): $0 to $1k MRR in 8 months, bootstrapped solo. Strong seasonal spikes (New Year). Other examples: $300 MRR in 7 days post-launch, $2.8k MRR accounting service seeking tech partner.
why_us: Shows viability of consumer + B2B micro-SaaS with persistence/launch tactics. Highlights co-founder needs and retention focus.
